Established · Private Membership

Pebble Pines

A Series About an Exclusive Country Club
×

"At an exclusive country club, a caddy witnesses the unhinged and entitled conversations of members who have forgotten that he has ears."

Format
Anthology Vertical Series
Tone
Dark Comedy · Satire · Camp
Comp
White Lotus × Caddyshack
Scroll
Visual Reference
Members Only · Est. MMXXVI
The Show

Tee Time 9am.
The Fairways Are Pristine.
The Members Are Not.

The Pebble Pines country club is home to one of the most exclusive golf courses on the east coast where the fairways are always pristine, memberships are inherited, and the conversations are completely unhinged.

The caddy says little, but sees everything.

This is a show about the absurdity of who gets to run the world.

Visual Language

Style & Tone

Tonal Comps
White Lotus
HBO · Mike White · 2021
×
Caddyshack
Warner Bros. · Harold Ramis · 1980
Visual Language

How the show looks.

  • Long lens, voyeuristic. Shot from the bushes.
  • Docu-style, observational. Feels unaware of the camera.
  • Hot mic audio. Crisp, intimate sound despite the distance.
The Groups

Meet Your
Playing Partners.

Group 01 · Female · 60s

The Karens

Swinging as intensely as they make dirty martinis.

Successful Gen-X girl bosses who close deals and close down the bar. They drink dirty martinis. They know everyone. And they will absolutely speak to the manager.

Group 02 · Male · 60s

The Boomers

Retired at 40. Still finds some way to talk about work.

Middle-aged white men with old money, but new money problems. Their corruption is transactional and deeply matter-of-fact. They pay for everything and understand nothing about value.

Group 03 · Female · Late 20s

The Passenger Princesses

More concerned about their status and looks than their stroke.

Women in their 20s who all wear $500 workout clothes. They discuss people and bodies with clinical cruelty. They're blissfully unaware that they are the punchline.

Group 04 · Male · Late 20s–30s

The Chads

Still running lacrosse plays in the boardroom.

Millennials who peaked in college and have been coasting on it ever since. Their corruption is financial and completely casual. They are genuinely confused when things do not go their way.

Protagonist
TheCaddy
The Witness · Male · Early 20s
He says almost nothing. Until he does.The Caddy, Every Episode
The Character

Hardworking, humble, and sharper than anyone gives him credit for. Carries the bags, watches the world unravel, and adjusts the strap.

Description

Watchable when still. A deadpan that lands without mugging. Reads the room in silence, then detonates it in a half-sentence.

The Series

Sample Episodes

EP 01
The Karens
The Manny
Ext. Hole 7, DayRuntime 0:45Script Locked
The Karens discuss their problems with their Au Pairs, and decide what they really deserve is a handsome man nanny to spice up their lives.
Script Complete
EP 02
The Boomers
Theresa from Hoboken
Ext. Back Nine, AfternoonRuntime 0:55Script Locked
The Boomers debate whether one needs an escort to have a happy marriage, until they realize they have all been seeing the same prostitute for years.
Script Complete
EP 03
The Chads
Pilk
Ext. Cart Path, MiddayRuntime 0:50Script Locked
The Chads discuss their favorite things until one Chad evangelizes his new obsession: Pilk, a Pepsi and milk mixture aged in a plastic bottle left in the sun.
Script Complete
EP 04
The Passenger Princesses
Ozempic
Ext. Back Nine, Golden HourRuntime 0:55Script Locked
The Passenger Princesses condemn the use of GLP-1s while systematically body-shaming every celebrity they can think of. A mysterious smell surfaces on the back nine. They blame the caddy. The camera reveals otherwise.
Script Complete
Talent

Casting

A mix of content creators, mainstream actors, and professional golfers. Cameo integration and cross-promotion across all three. A modern, exciting way for talent to show their love for the game.

01
Content Creators
Creators whose existing personas already live in this world. The casting itself becomes content, and their audience walks straight onto the course with them.
Shortlist
  • Becky Robinson@beckyrobinsonlive
  • Heather McMahan@heathermcmahan
  • Aris Yeager@arisyeager
  • Bob Does Sports@bobdoessports
  • Corporate Bro (Ross Pomerantz)@corporate.bro
  • Grace O'Malley@grace_omalley_
02
Mainstream Actors
Pairing actors with creators creates the chemistry and cross-promotion that shows like Brooklyn Coffee Shop run on. For these actors, this is a modern, appealing way to show fans their love for golf, and a fresh form of promotion through cameos.
Shortlist
  • Kate Hudson@katehudson
  • Tom Holland@tomholland2013
  • Adam Sandler@adamsandler
  • Theo Von@theovon
  • Bert Kreischer@bertkreischer
  • Bobby Lee@bobbyleelive
03
Professional Golfers
Entertaining tour pros already exploring content and cameos, the golf community already shows up for them. An exciting new way to see these personalities in a more laid-back, narrative format.
Shortlist
  • Bryson DeChambeau@brysondechambeau
  • Rory McIlroy@rorymcilroy
  • Rickie Fowler@rickiefowler
  • Justin Thomas@justinthomas34
  • Phil Mickelson@philmickelson
  • Paige Spiranac@_paige.renee
Field Notes

Golf is Having
a Moment.

The sport has never been more culturally relevant, more brand-hungry, or more underserved creatively. Pebble Pines is built for exactly this window.

Golf content is massively underserved creatively. The audience is affluent, brand-loyal, and starved for content that doesn't take itself too seriously. Pebble Pines sits at the intersection of culture and sport, an unusually natural fit for brands that want to reach this demo without feeling like an ad.

48.1M
Americans playing golf in 2025
Record high. Up 38% from 2019.
800M+
Global golf viewership (PGA + LIV)
And rising year over year.
21x
More engagement: creator vs. PGA Tour
Same audience. Fraction of the cost.
$7.6B
Golf sponsorship market in 2025
Projected $13.2B by 2033.
Participation Growth
Total U.S. Golf Participants
Millions of Americans, 2019-2025. Source: National Golf Foundation.

Of marketers say short-form video is most effective format

The Proof of Concept

Good Good Golf Proved the Model.

Good Good started as a YouTube golf channel in 2020. By 2025 they had raised $45 million led by Peyton Manning's Omaha Productions and are now title-sponsoring a PGA Tour event, a deal worth an estimated $12-15 million. What they proved: digital-first golf content is a serious business, not a hobby.

Pebble Pines operates in a lane Good Good does not touch. They are sports performance and community. We are satire and character. The audience overlaps. The competition does not.

$45M
Funding raised by Good Good Golf (2025)
1.75M+
YouTube subscribers across their channels
$12-15M
Est. value of PGA Tour title sponsorship deal

The Brand Play

Brand Integration
Strategy

A country club setting is one of the most naturally brand-integrated environments in the world. Every hole has a sponsored tee box. Every cart has a logo. Every drink order is a product placement. Pebble Pines does not need to manufacture brand integration. It already exists in the DNA of the world.

The golf audience is the most valuable demo in creator content: 74% mid-to-high income, an average household income of $101K, and deeply brand-loyal. Brands that reach golfers early and authentically build relationships that last decades.

$7.6B
Golf sponsorship market 2025
150%
Avg. ROI on golf sponsorships
48.1M
Total U.S. golf participants 2025
Endemic Golf Brands: Credibility Plays
PGA
The institution behind the game. Co-branded content, course access, and credibility at scale.
Parsons Xtreme Golf
The caddy quietly judges which clubs the Chads picked. Natural one-liner opportunity.
Puma / Rickie Fowler
Caddy fits as contrast to the Boomers' old-money wardrobes. Style credibility.
Good Good
Golf's most culturally fluent brand. Cross-promo and collab potential.
Barstool: Foreplay
Distribution and audience crossover. Clip licensing or co-produced episodes.
LIV Golf
Bigger purses, louder personalities, hungry for cultural relevance. A natural co-conspirator for satire that lives where the game is loudest.
Lifestyle and Resort: Aspirational Plays
Hilton Grand Vacations
Location integration. Episodes at HGV resort courses. Organic destination content.
We-Ko-Pa Casino Resort
Post-round scenes. The Boomers and Chads at the resort. Caddy not invited.
Arnold Palmer
The caddy's drink of choice. Understated, recurring, never called out.
Unexpected Brands: Cultural Plays
Golf by Tyler the Creator
Brand collision episode. The caddy's world vs. the Boomers'. Cultural tension as content.
Nature Valley
The caddy's snack in the bag. Wholesome contrast to the Boomers' charcuterie at the turn.
FootJoy & Shoe Brands
The caddy walks 18 holes a day. His feet are a legitimate story, and FootJoy is the obvious flagship.
Sources
